lessons.title: Introduction to Qubits
lessons.content: A qubit is the basic unit of quantum information. Unlike a classical bit that is 0 or 1, a qubit can be in a superposition of both states.

lessons.title: Superposition & Measurement
lessons.content: Superposition means a qubit can exist in multiple states at once. Measurement collapses the qubit to one of the basis states.

quiz.question: What happens when you measure a qubit in superposition?
options: It becomes a classical bit 0 or 1
